hard.next_step: Inviting to EY event in OCtober WIll be inviting to our EY event in October This is a long play deal - we are keeping them warm until they are ready for RFP Conducted on-site discovery and demonstration meeting at the customer's offices on 15th May 2026 with Rachel and James. Rachel showed strong positive engagement with the Zycus platform and Merlin AI capabilities. James has confirmed attendance at the Zycus procurement event next week — good opportunity to deepen the relationship in a peer environment. Rachel is impressed with what Zycus can deliver but has raised a significant concern around internal resource availability to support a procurement transformation programme. She does not currently have the internal headcount or bandwidth to run an implementation project. This is the primary blocker to near-term progression. Rachel's intention is to issue an RFP towards end of 2026. Zycus is being positioned to help her build the business case and shape the RFP — giving us a strong influence position ahead of formal process launch.

forecast_read.reason: Pipeline is the right category for a pre-budget, pre-RFP deal, but the 2026-04-12 close date passed 88 days ago and contradicts Rachael Coad's own stated plan to issue an RFP toward end of 2026; this is a date problem on an otherwise live, engaged long-play deal, not a deal problem. Reset the close date to the buyer's timeline (realistic close Q1-Q2 2027).
forecast_read.math: days_to_close = -88 (close date 2026-04-12 vs 2026-07-09); 73 days in Qualified (Qualified_Submission_Date__c 2026-04-27); CloseDate was pulled in a full year from 2027-04-12 to 2026-04-12 on 2026-05-12, the same day Amount moved 150,000 -> 450,000 (OpportunityFieldHistory), so the current date is a data-entry artifact; with the buyer-stated RFP at end-2026, no close is achievable before 2027 and pace-to-date has no meaning against the past-dated placeholder.

metrics.narrative: Rachael Coad quantified the scale of the pain on the 2026-04-10 discovery call: Greencore doubled in size in January 2026 via the Bakkavor acquisition to about £4B turnover, indirect spend is around £650M with roughly 30% in unmanaged tail across 2,000 live indirect suppliers (80% of them in tail), and there is a publicly announced £80M synergy target she says is 'not linked to any solutions at the moment' but that an S2P system 'would really help' with. No ROI or savings model has been confirmed as delivered back to her, despite Anthony Gray committing to a savings follow-up email after the 10 Apr call, so the quantified pain has not yet been converted into a business case she can take to leadership.
sources: SF Task 2026-06-18 (savings model still logged as overdue)

economic_buyer.narrative: No named economic buyer is mapped anywhere. Rachael Coad was explicit on the 2026-04-10 call that funding sits above her: 'The business isn't quite as enthusiastic as me yet, and part of my job... is being able to create a really compelling business case that I can go to the business with... and then we can just press the button and go ahead,' and that 'once we've got business case sign off, a whole team will be put together.' The budget owner is unnamed senior leadership; she referenced supportive 'conditioning conversations with the powers that be' (2026-04-10) but no person, title, or engagement. Mapping and engaging that approver is the live gap.

decision_criteria.narrative: Rachael set explicit demo criteria on the 10 Apr call ('It has to be interactive. It must be live... give us specific use cases') and named the focus areas: AI capabilities, savings-opportunity analysis, contract management, and supplier risk, adding autonomous sourcing to the 27 Apr agenda by email. Formal RFP criteria do not exist yet because the RFP is planned only toward end of 2026; Zycus offered an RFP primer on 10 Apr specifically to shape that requirements list, which is the influence position to press now. No documented decision criteria beyond the demo brief are on record.
sources: SF Task 2026-04-27 (Clari email, Rachael Coad use cases)

decision_process.narrative: Rachael laid out the process herself on 10 Apr: build the business case first ('I don't want to go to market first'), present it to the business for budget sign-off, then run the go-to-market/RFP toward end of 2026, after which a project team (project managers, IT, business analysts) is formed. The deal owner's plan records the same sequence being executed as a deliberate long play: discovery 10 Apr, on-site demo 15 May, keep-warm nurture including the October EY event, RFP at end-2026. The sequence is confirmed; the approver above Rachael is the unnamed piece (see economic buyer).

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been discussed on any call, email, or field: the deal is pre-budget and pre-RFP, so MSA, procurement channel, and legal review have not yet come up. That is expected at Qualified on a buyer-stated long play rather than a red flag; visibility will come once the business case is approved and the end-2026 RFP launches.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-04-10 (no paper topics raised)

identify_pain.narrative: Extensive, specific pain in Rachael Coad's own words on 2026-04-10: about 7 disparate ERP instances (Infor System 21 on 13 of 15 legacy sites, a site on legacy Great Plains, Microsoft Dynamics 365 on the acquired side, and a 5-year SAP migration just starting); standalone point tools that 'don't tie into anything' (Scanmarket for eProcurement, DocuSign as contract repository, Click for spend visibility that 'works well for directs, not well for indirects'); zero procurement visibility of purchase orders ('I've never seen a purchase order'); a site buying engineering day-rate resource from its nuts-and-bolts supplier; contracts historically in a SharePoint folder with repeated auto-renewals; and an unmanaged 30% tail. She is the clear pain owner as the procurement technology lead.

champion.narrative: Rachael Coad is a confirmed, strong champion: the deal team logged her as the established champion on 2026-05-12, and her own words on 10 Apr back it up: 'I will be running the project, and it looks like at the moment I will be owning the solution... I'm deeply invested... I'm like a big fan.' She stayed positively engaged through the 15 May on-site (owner log: 'strong positive engagement with the Zycus platform and Merlin AI capabilities') and replied personally on 1 Jun. Her constraint is authority and bandwidth, not enthusiasm: she must win over unnamed senior leadership for budget and has said she lacks headcount to run an implementation. James Dean is a secondary, less vocal co-lead ('James and I will be working on this together').

competition.narrative: No external S2P rival has been named in any source; the current field is Greencore's own disconnected point tools: Scanmarket (eProcurement), DocuSign CLM (contract repository), and Click (spend visibility), all described by Rachael on 10 Apr as standalone and weak. The real retention risks are Pactum, an AI autonomous-negotiation tool in its second year of use ('gets mentioned at every possible opportunity'), and a separate unnamed AI contract-redlining tool she says she 'loves'. The true competitive shortlist forms only at the end-2026 RFP; until then Zycus holds the shaping position on her business case and requirements ('working with you guys and with some of your competitors is being able to create a really compelling business case').

critical_signals.text: At the 15 May on-site in Tamworth, Rachael Coad was strongly positive on the platform and Merlin AI but said she lacks the internal headcount and bandwidth to run a transformation programme, the stated blocker to near-term progress.

No budget exists yet: Rachael must sell the business case to senior leadership before the RFP she plans for late 2026, and the savings model promised to her on 10 April has still not been confirmed as delivered.

No rival suite is engaged yet; Zycus is helping shape the business case and requirements ahead of the end-2026 tender, while two liked point tools (Pactum for negotiation, an AI redlining tool) sit inside the proposed scope.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early-stage, pre-RFP relationship with a genuinely enthusiastic and articulate champion and strong documented pain, held back by an unmapped economic buyer, a stated buyer-side resourcing blocker, and two open Zycus commitments (savings model, RFP primer) still unconfirmed as delivered. Steady versus the 7 Jul sweep: buyer replies on 1-2 Jun and a maintained keep-warm plan, no new buyer-side movement since. The single likeliest blocker to a close is budget sign-off by unnamed senior leadership, compounded by Rachael Coad's stated lack of implementation bandwidth.

win_position.text: Champion Rachael Coad is highly self-motivated and told Zycus on 10 Apr 2026 she is 'deeply invested' and expects to personally own the solution.
Pain is extensive and quantified: seven ERP instances, 80% of 2,000 indirect suppliers sitting in unmanaged tail, and zero procurement visibility into purchase orders (10 Apr 2026 call).
No economic buyer is identified or engaged; budget depends on an unnamed senior leadership audience Rachael has not yet secured (10 Apr 2026 call).
The deal is single-threaded on two contacts (Rachael Coad, James Dean) with no confirmed reach into IT, finance, or wider senior leadership.

deal_momentum.text: Genuine two-way buyer emails landed 1-2 Jun 2026 (Rachael Coad and James Dean both replied personally, James asking to be included in future events).
The last content-rich touchpoint is the 15 May 2026 on-site demo, about eight weeks ago; nothing confirms what has moved on the buyer side since.
Two Zycus commitments from the 10 Apr 2026 call (an RFP primer and a savings/business-case model) remain unconfirmed as delivered 13 weeks later.
The buyer has explicitly framed this as a long play with the RFP not expected until end of 2026, so the quiet stretch since May matches the stated cadence rather than an unexplained stall.

customer_commitment.text: Rachael Coad described herself as 'deeply invested' and a self-identified fan of the opportunity on the 10 Apr 2026 call, and was logged as impressed with the platform at the 15 May on-site.
Rachael flagged at the 15 May 2026 on-site that she lacks the internal headcount and bandwidth to run an implementation programme, the stated primary blocker to progression.
James Dean's engagement is still limited to event attendance and polite email replies; both contacts declined the 4 Jun EY session (1-2 Jun emails).

deal_risk.text: The recorded close date (12 Apr 2026) is 88 days past on a Qualified, pre-RFP deal and contradicts the buyer's stated end-2026 RFP; it needs resetting to the real timeline.
No economic buyer or budget owner is named; the business case and internal funding approval have not been secured.
Entrenched, buyer-liked point tools (Pactum, an unnamed AI redlining tool) could survive any eventual selection and narrow Zycus's scope.
Single-threaded relationship (2 contacts) creates exposure if Rachael's stated bandwidth constraint causes the initiative to be deprioritised internally.

customer_preference.evidence: At the 15 May 2026 on-site the owner logged Rachael Coad as showing 'strong positive engagement with the Zycus platform and Merlin AI capabilities' and being 'impressed with what Zycus can deliver'; on the 10 Apr call she reacted strongly to the savings-opportunity agent ('That's really good'). Positive but pre-competitive: no rival field exists yet against which she has voiced a preference.

business_case.evidence: Pain is quantified in the buyer's own words (about £650M indirect spend, roughly 30% unmanaged tail, £80M synergy target 'not linked to any solutions at the moment', 10 Apr 2026 call) but the business case itself is still being built: no ROI or savings model has been confirmed as delivered, and budget sign-off by senior leadership has not been sought.

deal_movement.summary: Steady, deliberate long-play cadence: Qualified entered 2026-04-27, Amount tripled (£150k to £450k) and the champion was confirmed on 2026-05-12, the on-site demo was delivered 2026-05-15, and the deal owner kept the plan current through 2026-06-30. Ownership moved Shriya Iyer to Anthony Gray (27 Apr) to Claire Hudson (13 May); co-owner Shriya Iyer was re-added on 2026-07-07, suggesting renewed account-team attention. No new buyer-side movement since the 1-2 Jun email replies.

items.change: Amount: £150,000 -> £450,000 (scope expanded to full S2P + Merlin suite)
CloseDate changed from 2027-04-12 to 2026-04-12 (same day as the Amount change; a data-entry artifact, not a real pull-in)
Owner changed Anthony Gray -> Claire Hudson
On-site discovery + demo delivered at Greencore's Tamworth offices with Rachael Coad and James Dean
Buyer email replies from Rachael Coad (1 Jun) and James Dean (2 Jun) declining the 4 Jun EY London session; James asked to be included in future events
Co-owner Shriya Iyer re-added to the opportunity

day_summary.overall: The Zycus side opened a fresh line into James Dean with a personalised email from Taniya Verma referencing their shared time at WPC London and pitching procurement excellence for food operations; a LinkedIn connection step followed the next day as part of the same cadence. This is rep-side outreach awaiting a reply, not buyer engagement. The last inbound buyer word remains 2 Jun, when James declined the 4 June EY session but asked to be kept on future invitations, a day after Rachael sent the same polite decline.

items.name: Outreach email to James Dean
items.summary: Personalised note referencing WPC London and procurement excellence in food operations, opening a second engagement thread into James ahead of the October event invite.

competitive_position.summary: No external S2P vendor is in a formal contest; the field today is Greencore's own weak, disconnected point tools. Scanmarket (eProcurement), DocuSign CLM (contract repository), and Click (spend visibility, weak on indirects) are low-threat displacement targets in Rachael Coad's own words ('doesn't tie into anything'). The real retention risks are Pactum, an embedded AI autonomous-negotiation tool in its second year of use, and a separate unnamed AI contract-redlining tool she 'loves'; both could be defended as already working unless Zycus positions full-suite consolidation. The true shortlist forms only at the end-2026 RFP ('working with you guys and with some of your competitors'), and Greencore's just-started 5-year SAP migration is background worth watching (inference: an ERP-native suite could be introduced by IT when the tender opens; no such vendor has been named). All competitor evidence is dated 2026-04-10.

competitors.name: Pactum
competitors.quote: Who do you use for that, Rachel, by round of interest? Pactum.
competitors.how_we_win: Demonstrate Merlin ANA autonomous negotiation integrated within one S2P data core, versus a standalone point tool, to justify consolidation; her team already cites the AI negotiation tool 'at every possible opportunity', proving the appetite Zycus can absorb.

competitors.name: Unnamed AI contract-redlining tool
competitors.quote: We've recently got a separate tool for contract red light AI contract redlining, which I love. It is the best thing ever.
competitors.how_we_win: Show iContract's AI redlining matches or exceeds the loved point tool while unifying it with full CLM and S2P data, so the tool does not carve CLM out of scope.

competitors.name: Scanmarket
competitors.quote: We've got Scanmarket for for eProcurement... a separate system that doesn't tie into anything.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus eProcurement plus guided intake on one integrated data core, replacing the standalone tool she describes as disconnected.

competitors.name: DocuSign CLM
competitors.quote: We're using Docusign as our repository. Docusign doesn't tie into anything.
competitors.how_we_win: Demonstrate iContract as a unified CLM tied into sourcing and spend on the same data core, removing the disconnected repository and the auto-renewal risk it has already caused.

competitors.name: Click
competitors.quote: From a spend visibility, we use click. Click works well for directs, not well for indirects.
competitors.how_we_win: Demonstrate unified spend analytics across direct and indirect spend where Click's coverage is admittedly weak on indirects.

competitors.name: Full S2P competitive shortlist (to form at end-2026 RFP)
competitors.quote: working with you guys and with some of your competitors is being able to create a really compelling business case
competitors.how_we_win: Own the RFP primer and business case now so Zycus sets the evaluation criteria before any rival engages; for this buyer, doing nothing means staying on seven ERPs and disconnected tools with an unmanaged £195M tail, which her own synergy mandate makes untenable.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Greencore's stated needs (one unified data core replacing disparate systems, AI-driven savings and tail-spend automation, supplier risk, CLM) map closely to the quoted full-suite scope (iContract, iSource, iManage, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin, iLogix, iRisk, Merlin Intake, iCompliance, ANA), and Rachael reacted enthusiastically to the savings-opportunity agent on the 10 Apr call. The one exposed criterion is delivery: she has said she lacks the internal resource to run an implementation, which Zycus has not yet answered with a delivery model.

best_practice_check.summary: A long-play, single-threaded deal where the strongest lever is deepening influence before Rachael Coad's RFP forms in late 2026, not chasing the lapsed close date: arm the champion, shape the criteria, and neutralise the two liked point tools before the field opens.
flags: Single-threaded on Rachael Coad and James Dean with no economic buyer engaged; her own plan to form a project team after business-case sign-off is the natural multi-threading vehicle, so convert it into introductions (budget approver, IT/data stakeholder) before rivals engage senior leadership at RFP time.

Zycus holds a rare pre-RFP shaping position (business-case co-authoring plus the offered RFP primer); convert it into named must-have criteria (agentic AI, tail-spend automation, one data core) before the end-2026 tender opens the field to competitors.

Greencore already likes two installed AI point tools (Pactum negotiation, an AI redlining tool); position full-suite consolidation early so those tools do not carve autonomous negotiation and CLM out of the eventual scope.

items.title: Senior Controller - Indirect Procurement
items.risk: sole visible driver, also logged as Decision Maker in Salesforce, yet final budget sign-off sits with unnamed leadership above her and she has flagged she lacks implementation bandwidth

champion_strength.summary: Rachael Coad is a confirmed, highly engaged champion who is personally driving the project and expects to own the solution; the deal team logged her as the established champion on 2026-05-12 and she stayed positively engaged through the 15 May on-site and her 1 Jun reply. Her constraint is authority and bandwidth, not enthusiasm: she must build the business case to win over unnamed senior leadership and has said she lacks the headcount to run an implementation.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus's AI story is landing: Rachael Coad asked unprompted about the savings-opportunity agent on the 10 Apr call ('That's really good'), Greencore already runs an AI negotiation tool whose success 'gets mentioned at every possible opportunity', and the owner logged strong positive engagement with Merlin AI at the 15 May on-site. The full AI suite (Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA) is in the quoted scope.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Greencore is demonstrably AI-hungry: two years into Pactum for autonomous negotiation, recently adopted an AI contract-redlining tool Rachael 'loves', and she says of AI negotiation 'I swear to God, gets mentioned at every possible opportunity. So, yeah, I think there is a real appetite' (10 Apr 2026 call). Her demo brief specifically included AI capabilities and the savings-opportunity agent.

items.detail: Rachael Coad stated she does not have the internal headcount or bandwidth to run a procurement transformation programme amid concurrent MBE transformations (supply chain planning, MES, time and attendance, a 5-year SAP migration). Logged by the owner as the primary blocker to near-term progression; no delivery-model answer from Zycus is on record yet.

contributions.detail: Confirmed, engaged champion — Rachael Coad logged as established champion on 2026-05-12, staying positive through the 15 May on-site and a 1 Jun reply.
No economic buyer engaged — MEDDPICC economic_buyer is only 'partial'; budget authority sits above Rachael and is unnamed, leaving the deal exposed to being outbid internally.
Pre-budget, no business case yet — Rachael must build and present the savings model to unnamed senior leadership before an RFP can even launch.
AI story resonating — Rachael asked unprompted about the savings-opportunity agent on the 10 Apr call ('That's really good'), and Greencore already runs Pactum + an AI redlining tool she 'loves.'
Single-threaded — only Rachael Coad and James Dean in play; no reach into IT, finance, or senior leadership.
Timeline slipping past the record — buyer frames this as a 'long play' with RFP 'towards end of 2026,' while the recorded close date of 2026-04-12 has already passed unchanged.
Dark for 55 days — last buyer session was the 15 May 2026 on-site demo; no meeting logged in the last 30 days and none in the last 60 beyond that one.
Close date already blown — recorded close of 2026-04-12 is 88 days past due with no forecast or date update in Salesforce.
Nothing scheduled forward — next_scheduled_meeting is null; the only forward mention is an unconfirmed 'EY event in October' invite, not a dated session.
Rep pushing, buyer quiet — the only recent motion is the rep inviting Rachael/James to an EY event; no buyer-initiated touch since the 1 Jun reply.
Champion still responsive — Rachael Coad replied 1 Jun 2026 and stayed engaged through the 15 May on-site, so the relationship hasn't gone cold, just quiet.

Close date already overdue — 2026-04-12 recorded close is 88 days past due with no update, while the buyer talks Q4 2026 RFP / realistic Q1 2027 close.

cro_panel.intro: An early-stage, pre-RFP relationship with a genuinely enthusiastic and articulate champion and strong documented pain, held back by an unmapped economic buyer, a stated buyer-side resourcing blocker, and two open Zycus commitments (savings model, RFP primer) still unconfirmed as delivered.; Steady versus the 7 Jul sweep; buyer replies on 1-2 Jun and a maintained keep-warm plan, no new buyer-side movement since.; The single likeliest blocker to a close is budget sign-off by unnamed senior leadership, compounded by Rachael Coad's stated lack of implementation bandwidth

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Send Rachael Coad the overdue quantified savings and business-case model promised after the 10 Apr call. (by 14 Jun)
Deliver the promised RFP primer so Greencore's requirements list is drafted around Zycus strengths. (by 17 Jun)
Propose a phased, partner-supported delivery model answering Rachael's stated lack of implementation headcount. (by 23 Jun)
